reasoning:
According to the resolution criteria, the market resolves YES only if two consecutive quarters within 2025 have a negative Advance (Initial) Estimate for Real GDP growth. The initial estimate for Q1 2025 was negative (-0.3%). However, the initial estimate for Q2 2025 was positive (+3.0%). This breaks the prospect of a Q1/Q2 or Q2/Q3 negative streak. The only remaining possibility for a recession within 2025 (meaning two consecutive quarters of negative growth) would be if both Q3 2025 and Q4 2025 were negative. 

The Q3 2025 Advance Estimate was released on October 30, 2025. The Atlanta Fed GDPNow model projected Q3 2025 GDP growth at robustly positive levels (around 3.8-3.9%) just days prior to the release. Upon the October 30 release, the Manifold market price dropped to 2%, strongly signaling that the Q3 Initial Estimate was indeed positive and effectively eliminated any mathematical possibility of a Q3/Q4 negative streak. Consequently, it is impossible to have two consecutive negative quarters of GDP growth in 2025 based on the initial estimates. The probability is constrained to the lower bound of 0.02.
